evals.prompt: This repo is still deployed on Deno Deploy Classic and uses Deno.openKv plus a custom domain. Please inspect the codebase, tell me what needs to change for the new Deploy platform, and give me a concrete KV migration plan. Don't just tell me to email support unless it's truly blocked.
evals.expected_output: Inspects the repo, references the official migration guide, identifies KV/custom-domain/manual dashboard work, produces app-specific KV migration guidance, and keeps support as fallback.
expectations: References https://docs.deno.com/deploy/migration_guide/ or explicitly names the official Deploy Classic migration guide.
Separates code-inspection findings from manual dashboard steps.
Provides a KV migration path with preview or dry-run behavior before real import.
Keeps support@deno.com as a fallback rather than the only migration path.

evals.prompt: Migrate this Deno app off Deploy Classic. It imports serve from std/http/server.ts, has a GitHub Action using deployctl, and calls Deno.Kv.enqueue for background jobs. Make the safe code/doc edits you can and report what is blocked.
evals.expected_output: Updates legacy serve usage to Deno.serve where safe, flags deployctl replacement, treats queues as unsupported on new Deploy, and separates code edits from manual or product-choice blockers.
expectations: Identifies legacy std/http serve usage as incompatible and recommends or applies Deno.serve migration.
Identifies deployctl as sunset and recommends new Deploy GitHub integration or deno deploy.
Identifies Deno.Kv.enqueue or listenQueue as unsupported on new Deploy.
Does not invent a queue replacement without marking product or infrastructure choices as blockers.

evals.prompt: I want an agent-operable runbook for moving my dash.deno.com project to console.deno.com. Use the repo wizard as the checklist, but adapt it to my app source and include verification evidence.
evals.expected_output: Uses the shell wizard as the codified flow, maps work to official migration guide stages, inspects source, and returns a structured runbook with completed work, manual dashboard tasks, risks, and verification.
expectations: Uses deno-deploy-classic-migration-wizard.sh as the local checklist or explicitly references the local wizard.
Maps work to the official migration guide stages such as env vars, domains, HTTP API, cron, queues, and KV.
Includes a verification section with commands run or commands that should be run.
Clearly marks dashboard-only work separately from agent-editable repository work.
